case_number: Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-5583 of 2023 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.25863/2023 (Urgent). 2. For order on CMA No.25864/2023 (Exemption). 3. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 22nd November 2023 Petitioner Mr. Nasir Ahmed Advocate present in person. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 3. After arguing the matter at some length and while confronted as to maintainability of instant petition, as prima facie it appears that no cause of grievance appears to have been expressed by the petitioner, which may require this Court to invoke the Constitutional jurisdiction under Article 199 of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan for enforcement of the fundamental rights, as the petitioner appears to have aggrieved against medical charges of eye and other treatments provided by Aga Khan University Hospital, Karachi, which according to petitioner, are higher and a poor man cannot afford such charges. The petitioner, who claims to be an advocate by profession, could not assist this Court, nor refer any law, whereby, such directions can be issued to a private hospital to charge a particular amount of medical charges, etc. for the purposes of operation, etc. Petitioner does not want to press instant petition. Accordingly, instant Constitutional Petition is dismissed as not pressed alongwith listed application. ACTING CHIEF JUSTICE J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
